description: Evaluation cases for ODD+2 narrative documentation skill

evals.prompt: I have a Python ABM with simple Agent and Environment classes. Generate an ODD+2 narrative for my model.
evals.expected_output: ODD sections covering entities, state variables, processes, stochasticity (if any), parameters, and initialization logic
success_criteria: Output identifies at least 2 entities (e.g., Agent, Environment)
State variables are listed with types and ranges for each entity
Output explains processes in pseudocode or plain English

evals.prompt: Create a timeline for documenting my project milestones
evals.expected_output: Skill does not activate; falls through to other capabilities

evals.prompt: I'm preparing a paper on an agent-based forest fire model. I need publication-ready ODD narrative.
evals.expected_output: Complete ODD+2 sections with validation feedback against the 23-point checklist
success_criteria: Output includes Overview, Design Concepts, and Details sections
Validation report notes any missing ODD+2 checklist items
Markdown is properly formatted and ready to paste into a LaTeX document

evals.prompt: My model code has no docstrings. Can the ODD skill still help?
evals.expected_output: Skill asks clarifying questions (entity names, state variables, process descriptions) rather than guessing
success_criteria: Skill prompts user for entity definitions
Skill does not hallucinate model components

evals.prompt: Generate documentation for my API endpoints
evals.expected_output: Skill does not activate; not related to computational model documentation
